_comment: Sprint manifest — read by regression.py R27. Set status to IN_PROGRESS when implementation starts. Archive to docs/sprint_archive/ on close.
_baseline_note: 437 confirmed entering (436 stable + 1 open-026 R8 addition). gov-regression-batch adds: R33 always-on (+1), R34 always-on (+1) → 439 always-on base. R35 fires at COMPLETE only (+2 checks when JSONL present). R27.struct (+3 IN_PROGRESS checks: token_budget, tier_a_ids, last_item). R27.complete (+1 at COMPLETE: wip_idle). R31.na_reason (+4 IN_PROGRESS). R30 always fires during IN_PROGRESS (18 checks for 9 items). Expected exit baseline at COMPLETE: ~446 (439 always-on + R27.complete + R31.complete + R32 + R35 — confirmed at sprint close).
_close_checklist: Manual steps at sprint close: (1) python3 sync_docs.py exits 0; (2) python3 regression.py → confirm PASS (exact count ~446, confirmed at close); (3) Archive manifest to docs/sprint_archive/.

items.title: regression.py — R33/R34/R35 new checks + extend R27/R31; enforce token-burn-log close gate and last-item rule
items.model_rationale: All additions are targeted string/structure checks and loop extensions on existing patterns — no architectural judgment needed. haiku handles mechanical assertion writing.
items.pass_criterion: python3 regression.py → 440/440 PASS. R33 fires if raw Momentum score string present in dashboard header. R34 fires if _ticker_cache_period missing from market_data.py. R35 fires at COMPLETE if token-burn-log item is not DONE or JSONL entry has null actual_tokens. R27 rejects manifest missing token_budget or Tier A IDs; rejects token-burn-log not as last item; rejects new sprint IN_PROGRESS when previous JSONL entry has no date_closed.
items.playwright: N/A — regression.py only, no UI changes
changes: Add R33 (always-on): assert no 'Momentum: X/100' pattern in dashboard.py _render_header_static section
Add R34 (always-on): assert '_ticker_cache_period' exists as module-level dict in market_data.py

Add R35 (always-on, COMPLETE-phase): when manifest status==COMPLETE, assert token-burn-log item status==DONE AND docs/ai-ops/token-burn-log.jsonl entry for sprint_version has no null actual_tokens fields
Extend R27 IN_PROGRESS: validate 'token_budget' key present in manifest
Extend R27 IN_PROGRESS: validate all 5 Tier A check IDs present in checks[] array
Extend R27 IN_PROGRESS: validate 'token-burn-log' item exists AND is the last element in items[] array (GAP-E)
Extend R27 IN_PROGRESS: validate docs/ai-ops/token-burn-log.jsonl — if file exists and last entry has date_closed==null, block new sprint from going IN_PROGRESS (GAP-G cross-sprint gate)
Extend R27 COMPLETE: validate GSI_WIP.md contains 'Status:' + 'IDLE'
Extend R31: require non-empty reason string after 'N/A —' when playwright field starts with 'N/A'

items.title: sprint-monitor.md + CLAUDE.md — Playwright hard gate + Phase 3 trigger review step (GAP-02, GAP-04)
items.model_rationale: Text edits to skill/doc files — protocol wording, no logic or architecture
items.pass_criterion: sprint-monitor.md Step 2a requires explicit user command (playwright-done or playwright-defer) before sprint proceeds to Step 3. CLAUDE.md Phase 3 close protocol includes snapshot Q-trigger review step.
changes: sprint-monitor.md: rewrite Step 2a as a blocking gate requiring '/sprint-monitor playwright-done' or '/sprint-monitor playwright-defer <reason> <PLAYWRIGHT-NN,...>'
sprint-monitor.md: add two new commands to Commands table: playwright-done | playwright-defer
sprint-monitor.md: add Step 2a handling block (parallel to Step 3 complete block)
CLAUDE.md: add Q-trigger review item to Phase 3 close protocol checklist

items.title: new-session.md — ACTIVE resume path writes minimal snapshot before resuming (GAP-01)
items.model_rationale: Resume-path snapshot requires judgment: what constitutes a safe minimal snapshot, how to handle partial deviation checks, and how to signal to the next-session reader that this was a resume-snapshot vs full snapshot
items.pass_criterion: ACTIVE resume path no longer early-exits past snapshot. Writes SNAPSHOT-NNN tagged RESUME with: regression baseline + sprint status + 'Full deviation check deferred — resumed from CHECKPOINT'. Proceeds to CHECKPOINT resume after.
changes: Step 1 ACTIVE branch: replace 'Skip the rest of this command' with 'Run minimal resume protocol (Steps 2–4 abbreviated)'
Add 'Step 1b — minimal resume protocol' section: run regression → write RESUME-tagged snapshot → then resume from CHECKPOINT
SNAPSHOT RESUME format: header flags it as RESUME type; Q01 only (baseline); all other Q marked 'deferred — resume session'; no deviation comparison attempted
After writing snapshot: proceed to CHECKPOINT block resume as normal

items.title: Token burn log: full policy wiring — CLAUDE.md Policy 8, enriched schema, sprint-open skeleton, aggregation script
items.model_rationale: Schema design (quality fields, methodology enum, session granularity), policy wording, aggregation script structure, and cross-sprint dataset conventions all require judgment. haiku would produce a mechanical schema; sonnet reasons about what data actually supports the optimization goal.
items.pass_criterion: CLAUDE.md has Policy 8 with full enriched schema (schema_version, actual_tokens_methodology, quality block, session_id, regression_runs_actual). sprint-monitor init creates token-burn-log task last AND writes JSONL skeleton on sprint open (IN_PROGRESS transition). docs/ai-ops/token-burn-log.jsonl exists with v5.38 skeleton (all nullable fields null, static fields filled). docs/ai-ops/analyze_token_burns.py exists, reads token-burn-log.jsonl, outputs markdown summary table of est vs actual by model/mode.
items.playwright: N/A — governance doc, skill file, dataset file, and Python analysis script only
changes: CLAUDE.md: Add 'Policy 8 — Token Burn Log' to Governance Policy Framework table with core rule: every sprint records est vs actual per item + quality signal at close

CLAUDE.md: Add full enriched token_burn_actuals schema template to Rule 2 (alongside token_budget template): schema_version, actual_tokens_methodology enum, sessions[], per-item quality block, regression_runs_actual, session_id
CLAUDE.md: Add 'token-burn-log' as mandatory recurring last item in Rule 2 sprint rules with enforcement note (R35 + R27 last-item check)
CLAUDE.md: Add token-burn-log.jsonl and analyze_token_burns.py to Living Documentation table

sprint-monitor.md: Step 1 (init) — always create token-burn-log Task as final task AND write JSONL skeleton entry immediately (static fields filled: sprint_version, sprint_name, date_opened, sessions[session_id], regression_baseline_entering, schema_version:1, methodology:'self_estimate')
sprint-monitor.md: PLANNING→IN_PROGRESS transition — add Step 0: write token-burn-log.jsonl skeleton before any implementation item

sprint-monitor.md: Sprint close Step 0b — fill all nullable actual_tokens, quality.rework_rounds, quality.regression_passed_first_try, wasted_tokens_est, overhead actuals, delta_vs_est, date_closed, then run analyze_token_burns.py to confirm parseable
docs/ai-ops/token-burn-log.jsonl: Create with schema comment header (schema_version:1, methodology options, field definitions) and v5.38 skeleton entry

docs/ai-ops/analyze_token_burns.py: Create script — reads token-burn-log.jsonl, outputs: (1) per-sprint est-vs-actual table, (2) per-model average accuracy ratio, (3) per-mode efficiency, (4) overhead-as-%-of-total trend, (5) waste rate by task type

items.title: observability.py — feed parsers: sprint manifest, risk register, compliance, snapshots, learnings
items.model_rationale: Parsing is mechanical: regex section splitters + JSON loads on known file structures. No layout judgment needed — all outputs are Python dicts passed to tab renderers.
items.pass_criterion: pages/observability.py has _parse_sprint_manifest(), _parse_risk_register(), _parse_compliance_output(), _parse_snapshot_history(), _parse_session_learnings() — all decorated @st.cache_data(ttl=300). Each returns a structured dict/list. R26 observability_no_yfinance still passes.
items.playwright: N/A — parser functions are backend Python, no direct UI interaction; correctness verified by tab renders in obs-P1b and obs-P1c
changes: Add _parse_sprint_manifest() → reads GSI_SPRINT_MANIFEST.json, returns {items, status, baseline, token_budget}
Add _parse_risk_register() → regex-splits GSI_RISK_REGISTER.md, returns [{id, severity, category, status, description}]
Add _parse_compliance_output() → runs compliance_check.py via subprocess, parses stdout, returns {gates: [{id, status}], summary}
Add _parse_snapshot_history() → parses ## SNAPSHOT-NNN blocks from GSI_SESSION_SNAPSHOT.md, returns [{session, version, deviations, q01_baseline}]
Add _parse_session_learnings() → parses ## RECORD-NNN blocks from GSI_SESSION_LEARNINGS.md, returns [{session, date, findings}]
All parsers @st.cache_data(ttl=300) — zero cost on tab switch, refreshes every 5 min
Add graceful fallback: if file not found or parse fails, return empty structure with error_msg field — tabs must not crash on parse failure

items.title: observability.py — Sprint Monitor tab: manifest table, token burn, next item card, git status
items.model_rationale: Tab layout requires judgment: column widths, status colour coding, token burn bar chart design, card component for next item. haiku produces functional but poorly structured Streamlit layouts.
items.pass_criterion: Sprint Monitor tab renders: (1) manifest item table with columns id|sub_sprint|model|mode|status|est_tokens|files — colour-coded status; (2) token budget burn chart (est vs actual where known); (3) next item card showing id, pass_criterion, model, permissions_required; (4) pending git commits list from git status. All sections collapse-safe (empty state message if data absent).
items.playwright: PLAYWRIGHT-07: Navigate to observability page → enter DEV_TOKEN → Sprint Monitor tab visible and clickable → manifest table renders with at least 1 row → columns id/model/mode/status all present → next item card shows a pass_criterion string. Edge cases: PLANNING status shows 'Sprint not started yet' card; all items DONE shows completion banner.
changes: Add 'Sprint Monitor' to tab list in render_observability() — after existing Program tab
Add _render_sprint_monitor_tab(manifest_data) function
Manifest item table: st.dataframe with colour map (DONE=green, in_progress=yellow, pending=grey, blocked=red)
Token budget burn: st.bar_chart or plotly grouped bar — est vs actual per item (actual=null shown as hatched/greyed)
Next item card: st.info() block with item id, model badge, pass_criterion, permissions_required list
Pending commits: subprocess git status --short, display as st.code block with count badge
Collapse-safe: all sections wrapped in try/except with st.warning fallback

items.title: observability.py — Risk & Compliance tab: risk heatmap, compliance gates, SEBI exposure map
items.model_rationale: Risk matrix heatmap, severity×category grid, and SEBI exposure map require layout judgment and colour design. haiku would produce a flat table; sonnet reasons about information hierarchy.
items.pass_criterion: Risk & Compliance tab renders: (1) risk severity×category heatmap — colour coded Open/Mitigated/Accepted; (2) compliance gate table — 9 rows, PASS/FAIL badges, last run timestamp; (3) SEBI exposure map — per-page disclaimer status (present/missing). All sections collapse-safe.
items.playwright: PLAYWRIGHT-08: Sprint Monitor tab passes first → click Risk & Compliance tab → risk heatmap renders with rows and columns → compliance gate table shows 9+ rows → SEBI exposure map section present with at least one page listed. Edge cases: all risks Mitigated shows 'No open risks' banner; all gates PASS shows green summary header.
changes: Add 'Risk & Compliance' to tab list in render_observability()
Add _render_risk_compliance_tab(risk_data, compliance_data) function
Risk heatmap: plotly heatmap z=count, x=category, y=severity — colour scale: Open=red, Mitigated=green, Accepted=amber
Risk drill-down: st.expander per severity level with filtered risk table
Compliance gate table: st.dataframe — gate_id | status (PASS/FAIL) | description | last_run
SEBI exposure map: hardcoded page list from CLAUDE.md scoped rules — show disclaimer present/missing per page using parsed observability checks
Loophole frequency: st.bar_chart of failure class trigger counts parsed from GSI_LOOPHOLE_LOG.md

token_optimisations.decision: Batch all regression.py gap additions into one item
token_optimisations.detail: 6 gaps all touch regression.py. One Read + one Edit + one regression run covers all. 6 separate items would each re-read the ~900-line file.

token_optimisations.decision: Batch Playwright gate + trigger review into one skill item

token_optimisations.decision: token-burn-log is haiku not sonnet — pure data entry at sprint close
